Patch Set 1:

please explain why do you need to pass namelen. also, it's not clear why RCU is used with no deletion flag by which the deleted records can be skipped.

Patch Set 1:

Patch Set 1:

namelen is passed in to avoid calling strlen(name) again for osd_oxc_add().

a deleted is not necessary because obsolete data can be tolerated in this case.

Patch Set 1:

well, let's same a thread sets some EA, then takes it again and return back (this is how we manipulate with LOV EA, for example). given the entry isn't marked properly, we would return a stale one? I guess you can set oxe_namelen=0 to invalidate entry?

Patch Set 1:

To let reader notice stale entry, the flag should be checked/set with spinlock, and the reader needs retry to read in new data.

I don't quite get your example, but IMO one thread set/delete EA, while another thread read EA, it's acceptable that the second thread read in stale EA.

Patch Set 1:

Onyx Autotest retesting enforced review-dne-part-2 session. Requested by Lai Siyao because of LU-8570

Patch Set 1:

no, I'm talking about the same thread. just as an example - LOD sets LOVEA, then MDT in the same thread gets it back to return to the client.

then, if you have a flag, then there is no need to reread anything as you just skip deleted entry and keep searching, right?

Patch Set 1:

after list_del_rcu(), should the same thread be able to find the deleted entry in the list?

umm, it looks like I should use list_replace_rcu() in osd_oxc_add() in case entry replace is not atomic therefore the newly added entry can't be seen in list traversal.

Patch Set 1:

i tend to think it's becoming too complicated and instead we just should use regular spinlock in all the cases.

Patch Set 5:

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

I think removed entries should be marked with a flag and then that flag should be used to skip entries. this is what I read in RCU paper long ago..

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 5:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 5:

AFAIU, rcu_read_lock() doesn't make any guarantee on state of a specitic record, it just ensures the memory pointers found on the list are still valid - because kfree() isn't immediate but scheduld to after rcu_read_unlock() point.

Patch Set 5:

Generally use of RCU means it doesn't matter a stale entry is used, if in a system stale entry is not permitted, a flag can be added (protected by a lock).

IMO xattr cache is in the former category.

Patch Set 5:

we must ensure that the same thread can't get a stale entry. like we discussed before: LOV EA is set by LOD, few CPU cycles later MDT fetches the same LOV EA to return to a client. I guess one option is to add new entries to the list's head always so lookup meets them earlier. but frankly I'm not sure why do we need to replace.

Patch Set 5:

ah, in theory the following case is possible: some layer deletes EA, but another layer in the same thread tries to access it right away. in this case we'd return a stale EA? IMHO, it makes sense to have an invalid flag to prevent such a case.

Patch Set 5:

I don't think the same thread can find a deleted entry.

e.g.,
1. reader iterated list to lookup entry, and it just scanned the first one.
2. writer added new entry into list head.
3. reader won't have chance to find the new one, but the old one, if the stale one is not allowed to use, then it has to lookup from head again.

This is why replace is needed.

Patch Set 5:

I believe if we add to the head, then replace is not needed - the subsequent lookup starting from the head will meet the new entry earlier. what does "replace" buy us?

Patch Set 5:

yes, subsequent lookup will find the new one, but concurrent lookup may not be able to find either new or old one.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

that makes sense. please, put a comment explaining why replacement is needed.

commit.subject: LU-8581 osd: misuse of RCU in osd xattr cache
commit.message:
LU-8581 osd: misuse of RCU in osd xattr cache

In osd xattr cache implementation several list operations didn't
consider RCU concurrency.

Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Change-Id: If4f91353fda5aca829f8e5ef327d9c06536f625d
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/22344
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
